---
title: Homomorphic ciphertexts
description: Apply homomorphic arithmetic to encrypted amounts with Ciphertext.
---

## Homomorphic ciphertext operations

**Focus:** one entry that creates a `Ciphertext` and adjusts it without decrypting it.

[Open in Playground](https://playground.xelis.io/?%3D%3D)

```rust filename="ciphertext_ops.slx" copy
entry build_adjusted_ciphertext(recipient: Address, amount: u64, fee: u64, bonus: u64, multiplier: u64) -> Ciphertext {
    require(amount >= fee, "fee exceeds amount")

    let encrypted: Ciphertext = Ciphertext::new(recipient, amount)
    encrypted.sub(fee)
    encrypted.add(bonus)
    encrypted.mul(multiplier)
    return encrypted
}
```

## How it works

- `Ciphertext::new(recipient, amount)` encrypts the starting amount for the recipient address.
- `encrypted.sub(fee)` subtracts from the encrypted value in place.
- `encrypted.add(bonus)` adds to the encrypted value in place.
- `encrypted.mul(multiplier)` scales the encrypted value by a plaintext multiplier.
- The returned ciphertext decrypts to `(amount - fee + bonus) * multiplier` for the recipient, without the contract decrypting it.
